name: Bertolt Brecht
description: German poet, playwright, and theatre director (1898–1956)
abstract: Eugen Berthold Friedrich Brecht (10 February 1898 – 14 August 1956), known as Bertolt Brecht and Bert Brecht, was a German theatre practitioner, playwright, and poet. Coming of age during the Weimar Republic, he had his first successes as a playwright in Munich and moved to Berlin in 1924, where he wrote The Threepenny Opera with Elisabe…

works_created.t: The Baden-Baden Lesson on Consent: Does Man Help Man?

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I really got introduced to theater through my experience with The Connection... one of the guys who was an actor in the play got mad... He was playing a Chinese bartender in one of Bertolt Brecht’s plays
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
